In Beirut, a school located near the southern suburbs regularly targeted continues to welcome students, while others attend classes remotely. How do you teach and learn amidst a war?  FRANCE 24's Serge Berberi and Renée Davis went to meet parents and students - and found some answers.

They would join thousands of Marines due to arrive in the region by March 25.

title: War in the Middle East: An opportunity for Iran's minorities?

Historically, they have often been ignored or mistreated by the regime in Tehran, both under the shah and the Islamic Republic. These ethnic minorities could play a role in the current conflict raging in the Middle East. FRANCE 24's David Gilbert and Florent Marchais report.

title: U.S. awaits Iran's response on peace summit as Israel watches warily
description: The U.S. and a group of regional mediators are discussing the possibility of  holding high-level peace talks with Iran as soon as Thursday, but are still waiting for a response from Tehran, two sources with knowledge of the discussions tell Axios.Why it matters: President Trump is interested in winding down the war, but Iran's chokehold on the Gulf of Hormuz complicates any potential exit strategy. The U.S. has shared with Israel its 15-point pla

title: US expected to send thousands of soldiers to Middle East, sources say
description: The Pentagon is expected to send thousands of 82nd Airborne Division soldiers to the Middle East, two people familiar with the matter told Reuters.

title: French economic growth undercut by war in Iran, national statistics office says
description: The French economy will grow by only 0.2% in the first half of the year instead of the 0.3% forecast, the Insee national statistics agency said Tuesday, citing higher inflation linked to the war in Iran. Inflation will surpass 2% in coming months, rising sharply from the 1.1% rate seen in February.

title: Is Iran's parliamentary speaker Ghalibaf negotiating with the US?
description: US President Donald Trump claimed on Monday that the United States had been negotiating with an unnamed Iranian leader whom he described as "very reasonable". Media reports have since speculated that the Iranian interlocutor could be 64-year-old pilot and parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf.

title: Anthropic challenges US Pentagon’s ban in San Francisco court showdown
description: Anthropic accuses Pentagon of unlawful retaliation over its refusal to loosen AI safety restrictions for military use.

title: Israeli soldiers accused of ‘torturing’ toddler in Gaza
description: The family of a two-year-old boy in Gaza says Israeli soldiers tortured him to extract information about his father.

title: Inside the alleged Russian operation to trigger anti-government protests in Angola
description: A group of Russians and Angolans are going on trial in Angola after being accused of fomenting protests, which they deny.

title: Iran names successor to security chief killed in US-Israeli attack
description: Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr, ex-IRGC commander, to replace late Ali Larijani as chief of Supreme National Security Council.

title: Afghanistan frees detained US citizen Dennis Coyle as gesture of ‘goodwill’
description: US State Secretary Marco Rubio welcomes Coyles release as positive step and thanks Qatar and the UAE for their help.
